fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Rhode Island Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Rhode Island - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity rhode island, how to transfer pharmacist license to rhode island, pharmacist compact rhode island, rhode island pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Rhode Island Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address:
Rhode Island Department of Health
Board of Pharmacy
3 Capitol Hill, Room 104
Providence, RI 02908-5097

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Rhode Island.

steps.title: Create an Account in eLicense Rhode Island
steps.description: Navigate to healthri.mylicense.com and click 'Register a Person'. Provide your personal details and create a unique username and password to establish your online licensing account.

steps.title: Start a New Application for Pharmacist Licensure
steps.description: Log in to your eLicense Rhode Island account. From the main menu, select 'Apply for a New License' and choose 'Pharmacist' as your profession. Indicate if you are applying by examination or by reciprocity.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application Form
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the application form within the eLicense Rhode Island portal. This includes providing personal information, educational background, internship hours, and details of any other licenses held.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Upload all necessary documents as prompted by the eLicense Rhode Island system. This typically includes official transcripts from your pharmacy school, proof of internship hours, official NAPLEX and MPJE scores, and possibly a criminal background check authorization.

steps.title: Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable application fee through the eLicense Rhode Island system using an accepted payment method (e.g., credit card) to finalize your submission.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check your application status via your eLicense Rhode Island account for updates. Respond promptly to any requests for additional information or clarification from the Rhode Island Board of Pharmacy to avoid delays.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Rhode Island pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Rhode Island have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Rhode Island. To work in Rhode Island, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Rhode Island?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Rhode Island while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Rhode Island
hero.dek: To apply for a Pharmacist license in Rhode Island, use the eLicense Rhode Island online system, where you can register an account and submit your application.

official_links.registration_steps: Go to healthri.mylicense.com and click 'Register a Person'. Enter your personal details (SSN, DOB, last name) and create a username and password. Verify your email to complete account creation. Once registered, log in to the portal to start your application.
